title: leptocallisia umbellulata
intro: Leptocallisia umbellulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, native to tropical Africa, commonly known as the 'African Boxwood Pilea'. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Leptocallisia umbellulata include its potential to reduce fever, treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and alleviate respiratory issues like bronchitis. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, with the leaves and stems being the primary components used in traditional medicine.
